channel.title: physics.pop-ph updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: physics.pop-ph updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Managing Uncertainty in Life Detection: Venus and Mars Claims Compared
item.description: arXiv:2607.17217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detection claims surrounding life beyond Earth often attract intense attention because the question of whether we are alone in the cosmos carries unusually high scientific, social, philosophical, and theological stakes. This chapter compares two high uncertainty life-detection claims, the 2020 phosphine detection claim on Venus and the 1976 Viking life-detection experiments on Mars, to examine how evidence, interpretation, and public narratives interact when results are ambiguous. We trace how each case unfolded through critique, reanalysis, and shifting assessments, and how institutional choices shaped subsequent research pathways, from long term monitoring and renewed Venus exploration to a Mars strategy centred on habitability rather than direct life-detection. Drawing lessons across both cases, we propose best practices for scientists, institutions, the wider scientific community, and science communicators, with emphasis on open science, constructive follow-up, cognitive bias awareness, and careful framing so that initial detections are not framed as final conclusions. We argue that managing future life-detections is less about eliminating uncertainty than about building transparent verification pathways that protect credibility as follow-up evidence refines the picture.
item.dc:creator: Daliah Raquel Bibas, Cl\'ement Vidal

item.title: The Physics Behind Ham Production
item.description: arXiv:2607.18173v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We analyze the physical mechanisms underlying the production of dry-cured hams, including prosciutto and jam\'on. Key processes such as osmosis and diffusion govern water removal and salt penetration, while enzymatic activity during long-term air curing develops flavor and texture without heat. The study quantifies diffusion depths, evaporation rates, and the effects of airflow and humidity on preventing defects like case hardening. By highlighting the physics behind salting and curing, the work provides a deeper understanding of how traditional techniques achieve safe, tender, and flavorful hams.
item.dc:creator: Sergey Parnovsky, Andrey Varlamov
